message.content:
Do a thorough audit of feature gating in /home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp. The app has 4 subscription plans: Free, Pro ($14.99/mo), Business ($79/mo, 3 seats), Enterprise.

Known plan limits from src/hooks/use-subscription.ts:
- Free: 10 Kira/day, 3 invoices/mo, 5 canvases/mo, invoice watermark, 1 seat, 1 workspace
- Pro: 40 Kira/day, unlimited invoices/canvases, no watermark, 1 seat
- Business: 200 Kira/day, unlimited, RBAC, multi-seat (3), clause library
- Enterprise: unlimited everything

For EACH of the following features, report:
1. Whether it is enforced on the BACKEND (DB trigger, RLS policy, or edge function check)
2. Whether it is enforced on the FRONTEND (component checks useSubscription(), shows upgrade prompt, or disables UI)
3. What file/line the enforcement lives in (or "none" if absent)

Features to check:
A. Invoice creation limit (free: 3/mo)
B. Canvas creation limit (free: 5/mo)
C. Kira daily message limit
D. Invoice watermark (free plan shows watermark)
E. CreviaLink profile themes (premium themes locked to Pro+)
F. Escrow payments (Pro+ only)
G. Workspace creation limit (free: 1 workspace)
H. Workspace seat limit (Business: 3 seats)
I. RBAC / promote-to-admin in workspaces (Business+ only)
J. Clause library (Business+ only)
K. Multi-seat invite (Business+ only — can you even generate invite links on free/pro?)

Search these files/dirs especially:
- src/hooks/use-subscription.ts
- src/components/studio/ (all files)
- src/pages/ (all pages)
- supabase/migrations/ (triggers, RLS policies)
- supabase/functions/ (edge functions)

Report format: one row per feature — Feature | Backend | Frontend | Key file:line

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me check the RBAC and seat limit enforcement migrations:

content.content:
1	-- ============================================================
2	-- Requirement 3: Strict RBAC – admin role locked to room creator
3	-- Requirement 4: Bulletproof single-use invite links
4	-- ============================================================
5	
6	-- ── 1. workspace_invites table ────────────────────────────────
7	-- Drop first so partial previous runs don't leave broken state
8	DROP TABLE IF EXISTS workspace_invites CASCADE;
9	
10	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
11	
12	CREATE TABLE workspace_invites (
13	  id          UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
14	  token       TEXT        UNIQUE NOT NULL DEFAULT encode(gen_random_bytes(32), 'hex'),
15	  room_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES chat_rooms(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
16	  invited_by  UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES profiles(id)   ON DELETE CASCADE,
17	  used_by     UUID        REFERENCES profiles(id)            ON DELETE SET NULL,
18	  accepted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
19	  expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT (NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days'),
20	  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
21	);
22	
23	ALTER TABLE workspace_invites ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
24	
25	-- Only the room creator can insert invites for that room.
26	-- Uses IN subquery so bare `room_id` resolves unambiguously as the new row's column.
27	CREATE POLICY "invite_insert_creator_only" ON workspace_invites
28	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
29	    invited_by = auth.uid()
30	    AND room_id IN (
31	      SELECT id FROM chat_rooms WHERE created_by = auth.uid()
32	    )
33	  );
34	
35	-- Any authenticated user can read an invite (needed to accept via token lookup)
36	CREATE POLICY "invite_select_authenticated" ON workspace_invites
37	  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL);
38	
39	-- Authenticated user can accept once; idempotent if already accepted by same user
40	CREATE POLICY "invite_update_accept" ON workspace_invites
41	  FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() IS NOT NULL)
42	  WITH CHECK (
43	    used_by IS NULL OR used_by = auth.uid()
44	  );
45	
46	
47	-- ── 2. RBAC on chat_room_members ─────────────────────────────
48	-- Drop all existing INSERT / UPDATE policies before replacing them
49	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Room creators can add members"        ON chat_room_members;
50	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_room_members_insert"             ON chat_room_members;
51	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_room_members_insert_rbac"        ON chat_room_members;
52	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_room_members_update"             ON chat_room_members;
53	DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "chat_room_members_no_role_escalation" ON chat_room_members;
54	
55	-- role='member' is always allowed; role='admin' only for the room creator inserting themselves
56	CREATE POLICY "chat_room_members_insert_rbac" ON chat_room_members
57	  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
58	    role = 'member'
59	    OR (
60	      role = 'admin'
61	      AND user_id = auth.uid()
62	      AND EXISTS (
63	        SELECT 1 FROM chat_rooms
64	        WHERE chat_rooms.id = chat_room_members.room_id
65	          AND chat_rooms.created_by = auth.uid()
66	      )
67	    )
68	  );
69	
70	-- Block any UPDATE that escalates a row to admin unless the updater is the room creator
71	CREATE POLICY "chat_room_members_no_role_escalation" ON chat_room_members
72	  FOR UPDATE USING (true)
73	  WITH CHECK (
74	    role != 'admin'
75	    OR EXISTS (
76	      SELECT 1 FROM chat_rooms
77	      WHERE chat_rooms.id = chat_room_members.room_id
78	        AND chat_rooms.created_by = auth.uid()
79	    )
80	  );
81	
82	
83	-- ── 3. accept_workspace_invite (idempotent SECURITY DEFINER) ──
84	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION accept_workspace_invite(p_token TEXT)
85	RETURNS UUID
86	LANGUAGE plpgsql
87	SECURITY DEFINER
88	SET search_path = public
89	AS $$
90	DECLARE
91	  v_invite  workspace_invites%ROWTYPE;
92	  v_user_id UUID := auth.uid();
93	BEGIN
94	  SELECT * INTO v_invite
95	  FROM workspace_invites
96	  WHERE token = p_token
97	    AND expires_at > NOW();
98	
99	  IF NOT FOUND THEN
100	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'invalid_or_expired_invite';
101	  END IF;
102	
103	  -- Already a member → return room without error (idempotent)
104	  IF EXISTS (
105	    SELECT 1 FROM chat_room_members
106	    WHERE chat_room_members.room_id = v_invite.room_id
107	      AND chat_room_members.user_id = v_user_id
108	  ) THEN
109	    RETURN v_invite.room_id;
110	  END IF;
111	
112	  INSERT INTO chat_room_members (room_id, user_id, role)
113	  VALUES (v_invite.room_id, v_user_id, 'member')
114	  ON CONFLICT (room_id, user_id) DO NOTHING;
115	
116	  -- Mark invite used only once
117	  UPDATE workspace_invites
118	  SET used_by = v_user_id, accepted_at = NOW()
119	  WHERE id = v_invite.id AND used_by IS NULL;
120	
121	  RETURN v_invite.room_id;
122	END;
123	$$;
124	
125	GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION accept_workspace_invite(TEXT) TO authenticated;

## Critical Gaps Found

1. **Invoice Watermark (Feature D)**: Flag defined but NEVER rendered - free users should see "Crevia watermark" on invoices but it's not actually implemented.

2. **CreviaLink Premium Themes (Feature E)**: All 35 themes are freely selectable by any plan. Pricing page claims "Pro+ gets premium themes" but no enforcement exists.

3. **Escrow Payments (Feature F)**: No subscription gating detected - appears available to all users.

4. **Workspace Creation Limit (Feature G)**: Free plan should be limited to 1 workspace; no backend enforcement exists.

5. **Clause Library (Feature J)**: Feature flag exists but feature not implemented/gated anywhere in the codebase.

6. **Inconsistency in Monthly Limits**: Backend edge functions enforce 2 invoices/2 canvases per month for free users, but use-subscription.ts defines limits as 3 invoices/5 canvases. Frontend UI shows 3/5 but backend gate enforces 2/2.

7. **RBAC** (Feature I): RLS policy protects role changes but there's no explicit subscription plan check - free/pro users are technically prevented from promoting via RLS, but this is database-level, not API-level gating.
